author: nc1234
content: <@592004585506340885> <@845691100072509460> Lodestar's been failing networking tests since alpha.5 and I think https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5050 is the cause because the state that is yielded is the post-state of slot 1 (by calling `next_slot`), not the genesis state. Given a genesis block, and a post-state of slot 1, there is no way we can start a chain without a genesis state. I think we should either revert this PR, or we yield the genesis state in addition to the post state although I don't know the purpose of the latter

author: nc1234
content: I think we should just revert 5050. Lodestar passes alpha.7 networking tests with 5050 reverted
